endpoints.description: Send a message to the Heady™ chat pipeline with optional archetype selection, memory injection, and streaming output via SSE. Accepts OpenAI-compatible chat message format.
Generate high-dimensional vector embeddings for one or more text inputs using the Heady™ embedding model. Supports batch processing up to 100 items. Returns embedding vectors and token counts.
Perform semantic vector search over a user's persistent memory store. Accepts a natural language query, optional namespace, top-K results count, and similarity threshold. Returns ranked memory nodes.
Spawn a new AI agent from the Heady™ agent registry. Specify archetype (owl, eagle, dolphin, rabbit, ant, elephant, beaver), task description, resource budget, and timeout. Returns agent session ID.
Returns real-time health status of all Heady™ platform services including API gateway, MCP server, vector database, Redis cache, and agent runtime. Includes response latencies and error rates.
Execute any registered MCP tool by name with structured input parameters. Returns typed tool output or a streaming SSE session for tools that produce incremental results.
Insert or update vector embeddings in the pgvector store with metadata, namespace, TTL, and dimension configuration. Supports bulk operations up to 500 vectors per request.
Search the vector store using pre-computed embeddings or a raw query string. Supports cosine, dot product, and L2 distance metrics. Returns ranked results with metadata.
Execute a named Heady™ pipeline — a composed sequence of agents, tools, and data transformations. Accepts pipeline ID, input payload, and execution options. Returns a run ID.
Retrieve the current execution status, progress percentage, partial outputs, and error details for a running or completed pipeline. Accepts a run ID as path parameter.
Spawn a bee worker from the 17-Swarm Matrix. Accepts bee type ID (from the 89 available bee types) and task payload. Returns a worker session reference for monitoring and coordination.
Returns the live status of all active swarm clusters — including bee counts, task queues, throughput rates, error rates, and φ-scaled resource utilization for each of the 17 swarm clusters.

Route a task through the Cognitive Swarm Logic (CSL) gate. The gate selects the optimal agent archetype, execution path, and resource allocation using φ-scaled decision logic. Returns routing decision and confidence score.
Record API consumption against a user or organization's budget allocation. Validates against φ-scaled spending limits. Returns current usage, remaining budget, reset schedule, and overage warnings.
List all API keys for the authenticated account with their names, creation dates, last-used timestamps, permission scopes, and rate tier assignments. Supports pagination.
